name: Langsuyar
description: The Langsuyar is a terrifying female vampiric ghost in Malaysian folklore. She is believed to be the spirit of a woman who died during childbirth or soon after, especially if she was beautiful. Transformation occurs if the woman's anger and grief are not properly appeased. The Langsuyar is known for her long, black hair that reaches her ankles, sharp claws, and piercing red eyes. She flies through the night sky, emitting a chilling laugh that strikes fear into the hearts of those who hear it. She preys on young children and pregnant women, seeking to avenge her own tragic fate. To prevent a woman from becoming a Langsuyar, needles are often inserted into her palms and glass beads are placed in her mouth after death. These measures are intended to prevent her spirit from rising and causing harm. The Langsuyar is a potent symbol of female rage and the dangers of unresolved grief in Malaysian culture, serving as a cautionary tale about the importance of respecting the dead and ensuring proper funerary rites.
subtitle: Vampiric ghost of childbirth
appearance: The Langsuyar has long, black hair that reaches her ankles, sharp claws, and piercing red eyes. In some accounts, she is described as having a hole in the back of her neck, through which she sucks blood. She often appears as a beautiful woman to lure victims before revealing her true, monstrous form. Her fingernails are long and sharpened into claws, and her skin is pale and deathly.
habitat: Langsuyars are said to inhabit forests, jungles, and abandoned houses, particularly near villages and settlements where they can easily find victims. They are most active at night, preying on the vulnerable under the cover of darkness.
behavior: The Langsuyar is driven by rage and a thirst for vengeance. She seeks to harm those who are living, particularly pregnant women and young children. She lures victims with her beauty or chilling cries, then attacks with her sharp claws and drains their blood. She is said to be able to transform into an owl, further aiding in her hunting.
weaknesses: Her hair (if cut, she is weakened)

keyMyths.title: The Origin of the Langsuyar
keyMyths.description: A beautiful woman dies during childbirth due to neglect or mistreatment. Consumed by grief and anger, her spirit transforms into a Langsuyar, seeking revenge on the living.

keyMyths.title: Preventing the Transformation
keyMyths.description: To prevent a woman from becoming a Langsuyar, needles are inserted into her palms and glass beads are placed in her mouth after death. These actions are believed to trap her spirit and prevent it from rising.

sources.author: Sir Richard Winstedt
sources.description: A classic text on Malay folklore and beliefs, including details on the Langsuyar.
sources.text: A classic text on Malay folklore and beliefs, including details on the Langsuyar.
